message:
feat(distributed): multi-block encoding with per-block repair distribution

StateEncoder now supports encoding objects across multiple source blocks
instead of treating the entire data as a single block. This is required
for large objects that exceed the maximum block size or when the number
of source symbols per block would overflow u16.

Key changes:
- encode() uses derive_block_layout to compute the number of source
  blocks and max block size, then encodes each block independently via
  a new encode_block_symbols method
- Repair symbols are distributed across blocks via distribute_repairs,
  which spreads repairs proportionally with remainder going to earlier
  blocks
- generate_repair_symbols() now tracks per-block repair counts and
  generates additional repairs starting from the correct ESI offset
  (existing repairs + source symbols), preventing ESI collisions
- calculate_params takes a BlockLayout instead of computing layout
  internally, ensuring encode and repair generation use consistent
  parameters
- block_bounds helper computes byte ranges for each block

Error handling improved: repair generation validates that the block
index is within bounds and that the exact requested count was generated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
